summary: eth-agent runs install-time credential and crypto-wallet stealer exfiltrating to hardcoded IP
details: On npm install, postinstall.js (also wired as preinstall) recursively walks the user's home directory looking for wallet and key material (id.json, wallet.json, keypair.json, keystore.json, seed.txt, mnemonic.txt, private.key, metamask.json, phantom.json, wallet.dat) and reads well-known wallet locations (~/.config/solana, ~/.ethereum/keystore, ~/.bitcoin, ~/.litecoin). It also reads installer-owned developer secrets: ~/.ssh keys, ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.git-credentials, ~/.npmrc auth tokens, and.env files. File contents together with hostname, username, and cwd are POSTed via HTTP to the hardcoded bare-IP endpoint http://107.161.90.180:7777. The package ships an empty description and no legitimate functionality — the entire package is the stealer, using the `eth-agent` name as a cover story.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
